entries.value: Target project gross margin is 20-35% for residential construction. Custom homes: 25-35%. Renovations: 20-30%. Below 15% signals estimating errors, scope creep, or material cost overruns. Track margin at 25%, 50%, 75% completion milestones.

Change orders should be <10% of original contract value. Above 15% indicates poor upfront scoping. Each change order should be documented, priced, and approved before work begins. Track owner-initiated vs contractor-discovered separately.

Target safety incident rate is 0 recordable incidents. OSHA recordable rate for residential construction averages 3.1 per 100 workers. Below 1.0 is excellent. Any recordable incident requires root cause analysis within 24 hours.

Target material waste is <5% by value. Industry average is 8-12%. Lumber waste target: <3%. Concrete waste target: <2%. Track waste by material category to identify ordering or cutting inefficiencies.
Target labor utilization (productive hours / total hours) is >80%. Travel time, weather delays, and waiting for materials reduce utilization. Below 70% signals scheduling or logistics problems.

Target schedule variance is within +/- 5% of planned duration. Variance above 10% triggers project review. Weather days are excluded from variance calculation. Track separately by phase (foundation, framing, MEP, finish).
Alert at 85% of budget consumed with project less than 75% complete. Critical alert at 100% budget with any remaining work. Require project manager explanation for any line item >15% over estimate.

Any failed municipal inspection must be resolved and re-inspected within 5 business days. Two failed inspections on the same item triggers a quality review. Failed inspections directly impact schedule and carrying costs.

Project managers must complete daily field logs by 6 PM including: weather, crew counts, work performed, deliveries, visitors, and any issues. Daily logs are the primary defense in disputes and claims.
Subcontractor work over $10,000 requires minimum 3 competitive bids. Document all bids received and selection rationale. Lowest bid is not always best — evaluate on quality, schedule, and references.
